nodes.name: Read Lead Sheet
nodes.notes: Range restricted to A3:R to avoid unnamed extra columns beyond R. Unnamed columns get internally named 'json' by N8N causing a conflict downstream. Execute Once ON.

nodes.name: Filter New Leads
nodes.notes: Must return cleanLeads (not newLeads). Strips rogue 'json' field from all items before returning. Without this, the Generate Opener node errors: A 'json' property isn't an object.

nodes.name: Generate Opener via Claude
nodes.notes: Mode: Run Once for All Items. Loops internally — one Claude API call per lead. Uses string concatenation not template literals to avoid N8N parsing issues. Never spreads sheet data. Output fields: row_number, business_name, instagram_handle, industry, status, opener, generated_at.

nodes.name: Update Lead Row
nodes.notes: Column to match on: row_number. Without this set, N8N returns: Could not find column for key 'undefined'.

nodes.name: Build Email Summary
nodes.notes: Uses string concatenation — not template literals. Template literals with backticks cause N8N parsing issues. Field names must match Generate Opener output exactly: instagram_handle, business_name, industry, opener.

changelog: 2026-04-07: Read Lead Sheet range restricted to A3:R — cuts off unnamed columns that create rogue 'json' field
2026-04-07: Filter New Leads now strips rogue 'json' field from all items, returns cleanLeads
2026-04-07: Generate Opener — mode Run Once for All Items, loops internally per lead, string concat not template literals, never spreads sheet data
2026-04-07: Update Lead Row — match column set to row_number (was undefined, causing 'Could not find column' error)
2026-04-07: Build Email Summary — full rewrite using string concatenation, field names aligned to Generate Opener output
